mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no significant negative correlation with rate levels.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate crossing above 4.5 percent signals a material cooling in the labor market, reducing household income growth expectations and triggering negative equity returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in more than 65 percent of triggered instances, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a broad contraction in consumer demand, forcing downward earnings revisions and producing negative S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint decline pattern yields a Rank IC below 0.01 over rolling 3-year windows, or the interaction term is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding 2 percent annualized reflects strong manufacturing momentum and capacity utilization, driving positive short-term equity returns during early expansion phases.
failure_condition: The signal loses predictive power or flips direction during recession regimes, with 5-day forward returns statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month CPIAUCSL growth surpassing 0.4 percent triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity risk premiums and generating negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 70 percent of high-inflation trigger months, or the threshold effect shows no out-of-sample persistence.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total payrolls and a rising unemployment rate indicates labor market fragmentation and weakening job quality, forecasting negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The divergence pattern shows zero correlation with 60-day forward returns, with a Rank IC below 0.01 across all rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth above 2.5 percent in chained dollars reflects robust aggregate demand, supporting near-term corporate revenue expectations and generating positive 5-day equity returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero across all GDP growth regimes, or the signal fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below its 12-month moving average while retail sales remain stable signals supply-side bottlenecks that inflate input costs, pressuring equity margins and returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The supply bottleneck signal produces positive forward returns in more than 60 percent of triggered instances, or the predictive alpha decays within 10 trading days.
